title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. hebridensis
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. hebridensis, also known as the Hebridean spotted orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues. The Hebridean spotted orchid has been traditionally used to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and as an anti-inflammatory agent, offering potential health benefits such as relief from bronchitis, asthma, and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. hebridensis, including infusions and tinctures, have been used to support respiratory health and soothe coughs, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
